fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: South Dakota Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to South Dakota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity south dakota, how to transfer physical therapist license to south dakota, physical therapist compact south dakota, south dakota physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: South Dakota Board of Medical and Osteopathic Examiners — PT
board.mailing_address: 101 N. Main Avenue, Suite 303, Sioux Falls, SD 57104

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in South Dakota.

steps.title: Create Portal Account & Start Application
steps.description: Visit the South Dakota Board of Medical and Osteopathic Examiners Online Application Portal (online.sdbmoe.sd.gov/onlineapp/Login.aspx), click 'Register Here', and follow the prompts to create your account. Once registered, log in and select the appropriate "Physical Therapist Application" (Initial Licensure by Examination or Licensure by Endorsement).

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your physical therapy program to be sent directly to the Board from your educational institution. Also, have your official NPTE scores sent directly from the Federation of State Boards of Physical Therapy (FSBPT). For endorsement, also arrange for verification of licensure from all states where you currently hold or have held a PT license, sent directly from those respective boards.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided within the online application to complete a fingerprint-based criminal background check through the South Dakota Division of Criminal Investigation (DCI) and the FBI.

steps.title: Pass Jurisprudence Exam & Pay Fees
steps.description: Complete the online, open-book South Dakota Jurisprudence Exam, which is accessible through your application portal. Pay the non-refundable $100 application fee through the online system.

steps.title: Board Review & Approval
steps.description: The Board will review your completed application and all supporting documentation. Once your application is deemed complete and approved by the Board, your South Dakota Physical Therapist license will be issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a South Dakota physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $100. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does South Dakota have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. South Dakota participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in South Dakota without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to South Dakota?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 4 to 6 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in South Dakota while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in South Dakota
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in South Dakota, you must create an account and complete the application through the South Dakota Board of Medical and Osteopathic Examiners Online Application Portal.

official_links.portal_name: South Dakota Board of Medical and Osteopathic Examiners Online Application Portal
official_links.registration_steps: Visit the portal, click 'Register Here', complete the registration form with your personal details, create a password, and verify your email. Once registered, log in and select 'New Application'.
